questions.question: What is the time complexity of Insertion Sort for an array of n elements?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort has a time complexity of O(n^2) because in the worst case, it needs to compare every element with every other element.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'decrease-and-conquer' approach in Insertion Sort?
options: To divide the array into smaller sub-arrays
To sort the array in ascending order
To compare each element with the previous one
questions.explanation: The 'decrease-and-conquer' approach involves comparing each element with the previous one to determine its correct position in the sorted array.

questions.question: How many comparisons are needed to sort an array of n elements using Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort needs n-1 comparisons to sort an array of n elements because it compares each element with the previous one and swaps if necessary.

questions.question: What is the space complexity of Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: The space complexity of Insertion Sort is O(1) because it only requires a single additional memory space to store the temporary variables.

questions.question: Is Insertion Sort suitable for large datasets?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort is not suitable for large datasets because its time complexity is O(n^2) which becomes inefficient for large datasets.
